close_reason: Added 13 unit tests covering every classify_bitwarden_sdk_error branch
description: src/lib.rs:211-235 contains seven different substring matches that each gate the Backend-vs-Unavailable classification. None of them have unit tests. The function takes 'impl std::error::Error + Send + Sync + 'static', so a test can construct a synthetic std::io::Error or a custom error type whose Display contains each magic substring, call classify_bitwarden_sdk_error, and assert it's mapped to the expected variant. If a future bitwarden-core upgrade changes 'connection refused' to 'connection rejected' the substring match silently breaks; a test asserting that the *real* error string format used by bitwarden-core 2.0.0 still triggers the transient branch would catch this in CI. Echo of crate-secretsx-bdu0.8 ('Azure: is_not_found / is_transient have no unit tests') — same gap, same crate-shape.
title: No unit tests for classify_bitwarden_sdk_error — substring branches uncovered
